name: Luna 17 / Lunokhod 1
first: First teleoperated rover to operate on another world
description: The first wheels on the Moon — driven from a control room in Crimea. Lunokhod 1 was a 322 kg pressurised tub mounted on eight independently-driven wheels, controlled by a five-person team in real time across the 2.5-second light-speed delay. It drove 10.5 km over 11 lunar days (322 Earth days), photographed 20,000+ scenes, and operated long past its 90-day design lifetime. Lost to history in the 1970s, located again in 2010 when NASA's Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter spotted it sitting where it stopped.

events.label: LAUNCH
events.note: Proton-K with Blok-D upper stage from Baikonur.

events.label: MOI
events.note: Lunar orbit insertion; orbital corrections to descent inclination.

events.label: ROVER DEPLOYED
events.note: Lunokhod 1 rolls down ramps onto lunar surface.

events.label: FINAL CONTACT
events.note: Last command session 1971-09-14; rover preserved in cold lunar night.
